title: persona-config
description: Copied to .claude/persona-config.json by install-antislop. The hook scripts read this at runtime via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}-relative scripts + a project-relative config path, so the plugin's hook scripts stay generic across every project.

testAndLintCommand.description: Single shell command the stop-gate runs; non-zero exit = BLOCK. e.g. 'npm test && npm run lint'

lintCommand.description: Formatter/linter invoked on a single changed file, e.g. 'npx eslint --fix'. The file path is appended as the last argument.

graphUpdateCommand.description: Code Review Graph's incremental-update command, taking a file path argument, e.g. 'code-review-graph update' (a pip/pipx-installed CLI, not npx - verify the current command at ADAPT time)

sourceGlobs.description: Shell glob patterns, PROJECT-ROOT-RELATIVE (not absolute), identifying source files worth graph-indexing. The hook normalizes the tool's absolute file_path against CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR before matching. If empty, every edited file is passed to graphUpdateCommand.

protectedPaths.description: Shell glob patterns, PROJECT-ROOT-RELATIVE (not absolute - e.g. 'supabase/migrations/*', not '/home/u/proj/supabase/migrations/*'), for paths requiring explicit human approval before Write/Edit. Examples: 'supabase/migrations/*', '*.lock', 'generated/*'

gatedAgents.description: Agent-type names the stop-gate's test+lint check applies to on SubagentStop. Defaults to just lead-programmer so cheap/high-frequency personas (explorer, scribe) never pay the check's cost. Add a persona name here if you add a new code-writing persona - this is a config edit, not a plugin file edit.

pluginVersion.description: The antislop plugin version this project was last ADAPTed/updated at, stamped by install-antislop. Compared against the installed plugin's current version by the SessionStart drift-check hook.

personaSelection.description: Which optional personas (hivemind, scribe, reviewer, researcher, milestone-auditor) this project chose to include, recorded by install-antislop' persona-selection wizard so --update mode knows which files to re-derive. orchestrator/explorer/lead-programmer are always included and not listed here.

issueTracker.description: Human-readable note on the tracker chosen during ADAPT, e.g. 'GitHub issues labeled plan/<slug>; fetch with gh issue list --label plan/<slug>' or 'local files in docs/plans/<slug>/issues/'

substitutions.description: Deterministic substitution values recorded at ADAPT time so `bin/cli.js --update` can regenerate a byte-identical fresh copy of each version-stamped file directly from the plugin's own source, with no LLM involved. Absent (or missing sub-fields) on projects adapted before this field existed - `--update` falls back to the LLM-driven `/install-antislop --update` flow for those, which also backfills this field.

mattpocockSkills.description: Maps each <MATTPOCOCK:slot> placeholder slot literally found in plugin source files (e.g. 'grill-me', 'to-issues', 'tdd', 'diagnose', 'improve-codebase-architecture') to the discovered namespaced skill name it was resolved to at ADAPT time, e.g. {"to-issues": "mattpocock-skills:to-tickets"}. Only needs entries for slots that actually appear in the selected persona files.

graphMcpLaunch.description: The code-review-graph MCP launch command inlined into explorer.md's mcpServers frontmatter at ADAPT time (install-antislop step 4, or `bin/cli.js --wire-graph-mcp`, which extracts this straight from the tool-generated project-wide .mcp.json). `env` is optional and only present if the server's .mcp.json entry had one. Null if the graph was never wired.

arxivMcpLaunch.description: The arXiv MCP launch command inlined into researcher.md's mcpServers frontmatter (install-antislop step 5, or `bin/cli.js --wire-arxiv-mcp <server-key>` once a server is installed). Null if researcher wasn't selected, or was selected but no working MCP was found (WebFetch/WebSearch fallback mode).

fileHashes.description: sha256 hex digest of each version-stamped file's content with its stamp line stripped, recorded immediately after ADAPT/--update finishes writing it. `--update` compares this against the project's current file (stamp stripped) to detect local edits without an LLM: matching hash means safe to silently regenerate at the new version; a mismatch means a human is asked before overwriting. Keyed by project-relative path, e.g. '.claude/agents/hivemind.md'.
